title: Alpha RC reconciliation and queue unlock
summary: Reconcile the post-alpha handoff before the next product phase: clean review-drop metadata, align queue and packet statuses, resolve the High Pass rime-footing copy/test seam, and add a strict alpha RC gate once cross-lane blockers are clear.

execution_order.goal: Prepare lane 1's reconciliation plan for review-drop hygiene, stale queue and packet status handling, archive/reduction safety, and the future alpha:rc wrapper.
execution_order.why_now: The post-alpha handoff is clean enough to continue only after the shared queue and release gate stop disagreeing with the reviewed alpha state.

execution_order.goal: Prepare lane 2's exact-copy and content-signoff plan, centered on the High Pass rime-footing mismatch and any stale alpha content references.
execution_order.why_now: The release gate should not be added on top of a known content/test mismatch, even if the broader alpha route and spatial work has landed well.

execution_order.goal: Prepare lane 3's alpha contact-sheet and visual proof reconciliation plan without reopening geometry.
execution_order.why_now: The reviewer saw strong spatial proof, but the repo should distinguish trusted source-complete artifacts from incomplete or generated review archives.

execution_order.goal: Prepare lane 4's route/support RC signoff reconciliation plan after lane 2's exact-copy risk is understood.
execution_order.why_now: Route/support proof is mostly clean, but final signoff should confirm labels and notices remain true after the content mismatch is resolved.

execution_order.goal: Implement lane 2's High Pass rime-footing copy/test reconciliation and final alpha content signoff notes.
execution_order.why_now: Content truth should be fixed before lane 1 declares a strict alpha RC command green.

execution_order.goal: Review lane 2's content reconciliation for exact-copy stability, science accuracy, and compact alpha-facing copy.
execution_order.why_now: The alpha RC gate needs a clean lane 2 signoff rather than a known mismatch carried forward.

execution_order.goal: Implement lane 3's proof-only contact-sheet/source-artifact reconciliation without new spatial density.
execution_order.why_now: The alpha spatial proof should remain trustworthy while avoiding another geometry pass.

execution_order.goal: Review lane 3's artifact reconciliation and confirm no visual-proof or source-completeness blocker remains.
execution_order.why_now: A stable RC needs a clean record of what visual proof is trusted and what generated output stays ignored.

execution_order.goal: Implement lane 4's route/support reconciliation and final notice-state checks after lane 2 content risk is cleared.
execution_order.why_now: The route loop should be signed off against the reconciled content state before playtest materials are produced.

execution_order.goal: Review lane 4's route/support reconciliation for replay labels, support behavior, and High Pass filed-state truth.
execution_order.why_now: The RC should not surface stale route guidance once the alpha arc has been filed.

execution_order.goal: Implement lane 1's review-drop cleanup, queue/packet status reconciliation, archive reduction, and alpha:rc wrapper after cross-lane blockers are clear.
execution_order.why_now: Lane 1 owns the final release/tooling truth once the content, spatial, and route signoffs no longer have blockers.

execution_order.goal: Review lane 1's final RC reconciliation and confirm the queue is unlocked for playtest packet 159 rather than Source to Shore implementation.
execution_order.why_now: The project should leave this packet with a clean alpha RC gate, not another deadlocked queue or premature beta campaign.

owns: strict alpha:rc wrapper after other lane blockers clear
route/support final signoff after content reconciliation

completion_gate: Review-drop archives omit node_modules, dist, output, .git, .tmp, and macOS AppleDouble metadata.
Queue and packet statuses for packets 149-157 are reconciled or explicitly deferred with no silent READY mismatch.
The High Pass rime-footing copy/test mismatch is resolved or explicitly documented as non-blocking with passing checks.
npm run validate:agents passes with no errors.
Relevant content, route, runtime, build, and alpha RC checks pass before packet 158 is marked DONE.
The work queue has an actionable next packet 159 playtest start or a documented blocker; Source to Shore packets remain blocked until the playtest gate.
guardrails: Do not begin Source to Shore implementation in packet 158.
Do not add biome six, season three, combat, survival meters, crafting, inventory sprawl, or a broader planner shell.
Do not reopen High Pass geometry density unless a lane-3 proof shows a release-blocking visual issue.
Keep the 256x160 handheld screen budget protected; prefer reports, tests, and release hygiene over new text surfaces.
Respect lane ownership and the 2026-04-21 lane collision rules for shared files.
Generated archives and browser proof output should stay ignored and out of commits.
post_packet_path: Packet 159 should create the alpha playtest kit and observation protocol.
Packet 160 should triage playtest feedback and decide whether Source to Shore opens.
Packets 161-176 remain seeded beta-campaign material until the playtest gate is clean.
